data.title: FSB Chair Urges Global Regulatory Cooperation on Stablecoins and Private Credit
data.description: According to PANews, Andrew Bailey, the Chair of the Financial Stability Board (FSB), has called for enhanced global regulatory collaboration in response to the rapid growth of private credit markets and stablecoins. In a letter to G20 leaders, Bailey warned that differences in national regulatory and prudential frameworks for stablecoins could heighten systemic risks. He advocated for the establishment of cross-border compliance mechanisms to address these challenges. Additionally, Bailey highlighted the slow progress of major economies in implementing the Basel III global banking capital standards, urging attention to this issue.

data.title: The probability of the Federal Reserve cutting interest rates by 25 basis points in December is 39.6%.
data.description: According to CME's "FedWatch": the probability of the Federal Reserve cutting interest rates by 25 basis points in December is 39.6%, and the probability of keeping rates unchanged is 60.4%. The probability of the Fed cutting rates by a cumulative total of 25 basis points by January next year is 50.2%, the probability of keeping rates unchanged is 29.7%, and the probability of a cumulative rate cut of 50 basis points is 20.2%.

data.title: U.S. Stock Market Declines as Cryptocurrency Faces Downward Pressure
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, U.S. stock markets closed lower on Thursday, with the Dow Jones Industrial Average dropping 0.84%, the S&P 500 falling 1.55%, and the Nasdaq Composite decreasing by 2.15%. Nvidia shares fell 3.1% after initially rising by 5% during the trading session.

In the cryptocurrency market, Bitcoin briefly fell to a low of $86,100 at 3:00 AM UTC+8, before slightly recovering to $87,200. Ethereum also dipped below $2,800, later rebounding to $2,850.

The recent large-scale outflows from spot Bitcoin and Ethereum ETFs, coupled with the weakness in U.S. stocks, have contributed to the downward pressure on the cryptocurrency market. Bitcoin tends to decline during U.S. trading hours and then recover during the Asian trading session, highlighting the selling pressure from the U.S.

Additionally, the market no longer anticipates a Federal Reserve rate cut in December. Strong employment figures have reduced the risk of rising unemployment, with the probability of a 25 basis point rate cut by the Federal Reserve in December standing at 39.6%.

data.title: SEC's Jamie Selway Discusses Crypto Market Structure at SIFMA Conference
data.description: According to Odaily, Jamie Selway, Director of the Division of Trading and Markets at the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC), addressed the SIFMA Market Structure Conference, highlighting the paradox of decentralized crypto assets relying on a trust-based market structure for healthy trading. Selway emphasized that the SEC's 'Project Crypto' aims to establish clear regulatory guidelines to foster competition and innovation while preventing regulatory distortions that could affect fair market competition. He stressed the importance of fair treatment for both new and established market participants, asserting that the market itself is the ultimate arbiter of value.

data.title: Director of the Trading and Markets Division of the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission: "Trustless" mechanisms for digital assets need to operate in "trusted" markets.
data.description: Jamie Selway, Director of the Trading and Markets Division of the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC), stated at the SIFMA Market Structure Conference that although crypto assets are built on a "trustless" decentralized mechanism, healthy trading still depends on a market structure based on "trust." Selway pointed out that the SEC will promote clear regulatory rules through "Project Crypto," supporting competition and innovation, and avoiding regulatory distortions that could distort fair market competition. He emphasized that policymakers should treat all market participants, old and new, fairly, and that the market itself is the ultimate arbiter of value.

data.title: Kalshi raises $1 billion in funding, led by Sequoia and CapitalG.
data.description: According to TechCrunch, citing sources familiar with the matter, prediction market platform Kalshi has completed a $1 billion funding round, valuing the company at $11 billion. The round was led by Sequoia and CapitalG. This funding round comes less than two months after its previous $300 million round, valuing the company at $5 billion. Kalshi allows users to place bets on various events and operates within legal boundaries, with annualized trading volume exceeding $50 billion. Its main competitor, Polymarket, is also reportedly planning to raise funds at a valuation of $12-15 billion.

data.title: A change in the US government's address led to the transfer of assets seized by FTX-Alameda and Bitfinex hackers to a new wallet, worth over $12.75 million.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, the US government has transferred the following seized funds to new wallets today, including: FTX-Alameda related wallet assets: - 15,131,604 TRX, worth $4.2 million; - 545,095 FTT, worth $3.4894 million; - 744,213 KNC, worth $2.0685 million; - 273 FET, worth $84. Bitfinex hack related assets: - 1,066 WETH, worth $3.01 million.

data.title: A scientist launched a bid for Jesse tokens, spending $40,000 in gas fees, and received a return of approximately $1 million.
data.description: 0xAA, founder of WTF Academy, posted on the X platform that the Jesse token, issued by Jesse Pollak, the lead developer of the Base protocol, was targeted by a "scientist" attack at its opening. One address used 67 ETH to buy 7.6% of the total Jesse token supply, becoming the first address to do so, incurring gas fees of $40,000. Their average cost per share corresponded to a market value of $2.4 million. They subsequently liquidated their holdings when the market value of the Jesse token exceeded $10 million, making a profit of approximately $1 million.

data.title: Brother Machi deposited another $115,000 to go long on ETH.
data.description: According to analyst Yu Jin's monitoring, "Brother Maji" persisted in opening long positions despite the continuous decline in the ETH market, resulting in daily liquidation for over a week followed by deposits to add to his positions, creating a cycle of "opening long positions → being liquidated → depositing more money to open long positions → being liquidated again." His most recent long position in ETH, opened after depositing 115,000 USDT in the early morning, was again liquidated, resulting in a loss of 775 ETH (approximately 20% of his position).

data.title: US stocks closed: Nasdaq fell more than 2%, Nvidia opened higher but closed lower.
data.description: U.S. stocks closed lower on Thursday, with the Dow Jones Industrial Average down 0.84%, the S&P 500 down 1.55%, and the Nasdaq Composite down 2.15%. Nvidia (NVDA.O) fell 3.1%, after rising as much as 5% during the session, Micron Technology (MU.O) fell more than 10%, and Oracle (ORCL.N) fell more than 6%. Blockchain concept stocks generally declined, with Coinbase falling more than 7% and Circle falling about 4%.

data.title: AVAX One's board of directors approved a stock buyback program of up to $40 million.
data.description: Nasdaq-listed AVAX One announced that its board of directors has approved a one-year share buyback program of up to $40 million. CEO Jolie Kahn stated that the buyback is an efficient use of capital at the current share price level, demonstrating confidence in the company's long-term strategy and market value. AVAX One focuses on connecting the Avalanche ecosystem with traditional finance, aiming to build a digital asset vault and drive DeFi innovation.

data.title: In the past 24 hours, approximately $831 million in margin calls were triggered across all trading platforms, affecting over 227,000 people globally.
data.description: According to Coinglass data, approximately $831 million in liquidations occurred across the entire cryptocurrency exchange in the past 24 hours, with SOL accounting for about $36.3753 million of those liquidations. The largest single liquidation occurred on the HTX BTC-USDT trading pair, worth a staggering $30.9176 million. Globally, over 227,000 people have experienced liquidations.

data.description:

data.title: Bitmine Acquires Additional Ethereum Holdings Worth $49 Million
data.description: According to PANews, Bitmine has expanded its Ethereum holdings by purchasing 17,242 ETH from FalconX and BitGo. The acquisition is valued at approximately $49.07 million based on current market prices, as reported by Onchain Lens.

data.title: Coinbase Rolls Out Million-Dollar ETH Loans As Base Lending Surges
data.description:
Coinbase is pushing deeper into onchain finance with the launch of Ethereum-backed loans for eligible U. S. customers, a move that lets users borrow up to $1 million in USDC without selling a single unit of ETH
source: https://www.ethnews.com/coinbase-rolls-out-million-dollar-eth-loans-as-base-lending-surges/
